title: signalk-notification-dispatcher ruleset
description: Shape of the ruleset persisted as ruleset.json and exchanged via the GET/PUT /ruleset REST endpoint and the webapp's JSON import/export/editor. Modeled like an iptables firewall chain: an ordered list of rules, each with a match block and a target, plus a chain-level default policy applied when no rule matches.

policy.description: Default target applied when no rule in the chain matches a notification.

rules.description: Ordered list of filter rules, evaluated top to bottom. The first matching rule decides the outcome; if none match, `policy` applies.

policyTarget.description: A chain-level default target: ACCEPT (forward) or DROP (suppress). Unlike a rule's target, the default policy cannot be MODIFY.

timeboxEntry.description: Either a simple 24h UTC anchor time ("HH:MM"), or a standard 5-field crontab expression ("minute hour dom month dow", UTC) for expert use, e.g. "15 2,6,10,14,18,22 * * *".

timebox.description: Optional time-of-day restriction on a rule. When enabled, the rule only matches within +/- toleranceMinutes of one of the given anchor times (handles wraparound across midnight).

toleranceMinutes.description: Tolerance window, in minutes, applied on either side of each anchor time.

vesselState.description: Per-rule gate based on the own vessel's navigation.state. Independent toggles; either or both can be enabled. If the own vessel is currently in a state with its toggle enabled, the rule is skipped as if it hadn't matched (evaluation falls through to the next rule, or to the default policy).

match.description: The conditions a notification must meet for this rule to apply. All conditions must hold (logical AND).

path.description: Glob pattern matched against the notification path with the leading "notifications." stripped. "*" matches any run of characters, including "." (so "navigation.anchor*" matches "navigation.anchorDrag"). "*" alone or an empty string matches everything.

vessel.description: Glob pattern (same wildcard rules as `path`) matched against either the notification's source vessel MMSI/label or its full SignalK context string. "*" matches any vessel.

states.description: Notification states that trigger this rule.

alwaysAcceptNormal.description: When true, this rule also matches state transitions to "nominal" or "normal" regardless of the `states` list above - so a rule narrowly scoped to e.g. alarm/emergency can still catch a source returning to normal, without needing nominal/normal added to its main severity filter.

modify.description: Only meaningful when the rule's target is MODIFY: overrides applied to the notification before it's forwarded. Currently supports overriding `state`; extend here if more fields need to be modifiable later.

state.description: Overrides the notification's own `state` while forwarding it, e.g. downgrading a recurring securité broadcast from alarm to warn instead of dropping it outright. null means no override.

action.description: Only meaningful when the rule's target is ACTION: writes a value to an arbitrary Signal K path, or calls an arbitrary URL, as a side effect - independent of whether the notification itself gets forwarded (see `forward`).

mode.description: How the write happens. 'delta' merges the value into the data model, the same mechanism ACCEPT/MODIFY use to forward - works for any Signal K path, but isn't a real command. 'put' issues an actual PUT request against the own vessel - only works for paths with a registered put handler (e.g. switches), but is a real actuation. 'rest' calls an arbitrary URL over HTTP - not scoped to Signal K at all, for calling out to other services (e.g. a Node-RED flow or Home Assistant webhook); see `method`.

path.description: Target Signal K path to write to (own vessel), for 'delta'/'put' mode - or the full URL to call, for 'rest' mode. Plain text; may contain {ref} placeholders - see `value`.

value.description: Value to write ('delta'/'put' mode), or the request body sent for PUT/POST ('rest' mode, ignored for GET) - as plain text. May contain {ref} placeholders: {vessel}/{path}/{uuid} as usual, or a dot-path into the triggering notification's own value object (e.g. {state}, {status.isAcknowledged}). A template that's entirely a single placeholder resolves to that reference's own type (boolean/number/object/etc.); otherwise placeholders are stringified into the surrounding text. After placeholder resolution, a string result gets one extra JSON.parse pass as type coercion - "true"/"42"/'{"a":1}' become their real types, anything not valid JSON (e.g. "on") stays a plain string.

method.description: HTTP method used when mode is 'rest'. Ignored for 'delta'/'put'.

forward.description: Whether to also forward the notification via the normal targetPathTemplate mechanism, same as ACCEPT would (with the same {uuid}-per-instance path reuse and clear-tracking).

id.description: Stable identifier, generated server-side if omitted on creation/import.

label.description: Optional human-readable label shown in the webapp and activity log instead of the id.

enabled.description: Disabled rules are skipped entirely during matching.

target.description: What happens to a notification this rule matches: ACCEPT (forward), MODIFY (forward with an override applied first, see `modify`), ACTION (write an arbitrary value to an arbitrary path, see `action`), or DROP (suppress silently).

targetPathTemplate.description: Used when target is ACCEPT or MODIFY, and optionally by ACTION when action.forward is true. Template for the forwarded path under vessels.self.notifications.*, with {vessel}, {path}, and {uuid} placeholders. Each {uuid} is replaced with a freshly-generated random UUID at forward time (a distinct value per occurrence).
